close_reason: Positive observation: Zeroizing token with documented gaps is correct
description:
src/lib.rs:111 'access_token: Zeroizing<String>' wraps the env-var-derived token. Lib doc lines 48-58 explicitly enumerate what is and is not zeroed: 'access token is stored as Zeroizing<String> and zeroed when the backend is dropped. The raw HTTP response buffer from reqwest (bytes::Bytes) is not zeroed on drop ... [explanation] ... only the final SecretValue produced by get is zeroed on drop.'

This kind of explicit gap-disclosure is rare — most secret-handling crates either claim full zeroization (overpromise) or skip the topic. The doc accurately bounds what the user can rely on.

A contributor might:
  - Remove Zeroizing in favor of plain String 'because reqwest can't zero anyway' -> loses the one zeroization step we DO get (the env-var copy in process memory between construction and drop).
  - Add a misleading 'fully zeroed' claim -> overpromise.

Defense: Zeroizing<String> for the long-lived token is the right floor. The honest documentation about response-buffer and bearer_auth gaps is the right ceiling. There is a separate priority-2 finding to extend the doc to mention the bearer_auth header-value copy too.
title: Defend: Zeroizing<String> for access_token + documented zeroization gaps
